text: Well done is better than well said.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop.
author: Confucius

text: Well begun is half done.
author: Aristotle

text: You have power over your mind — not outside events.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Learning never exhausts the mind.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: Genius is one percent inspiration and ninety-nine percent perspiration.
author: Thomas Edison

text: I go with the idea that the future depends on what we do in the present.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: Energy and persistence conquer all things.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: Diligence is the mother of good luck.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: I am a slow walker, but I never walk back.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones.
author: Confucius

text: Great works are performed not by strength but by perseverance.
author: Samuel Johnson

text: Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.
author: Seneca

text: No great thing is created suddenly.
author: Epictetus

text: Opportunity is missed by most people because it is dressed in overalls and looks like work.
author: Thomas Edison

text: Nothing in the world can take the place of persistence.
author: Calvin Coolidge

text: Knowing is not enough; we must apply. Willing is not enough; we must do.
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

text: By failing to prepare, you are preparing to fail.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: Perseverance and spirit have done wonders in all ages.
author: George Washington

text: It is by diligence, not by strength, that great things are accomplished.
author: Samuel Johnson

text: Concentrate all your thoughts upon the work in hand.
author: Alexander Graham Bell

text: The expectations of life depend upon diligence; the mechanic that would perfect his work must first sharpen his tools.
author: Confucius

text: Without labor nothing prospers.
author: Sophocles

text: Whatever is worth doing at all is worth doing well.
author: Philip Stanhope, 4th Earl of Chesterfield

text: Nothing great was ever achieved without enthusiasm.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Either write something worth reading, or do something worth writing.
author: Benjamin Franklin
